title: Exercises: Cloud Quantum Computing Cost Optimization

exercises.title: Multi-Provider Cost Calculator
exercises.description: Build a comprehensive cost calculator that compares pricing across all major quantum cloud providers.
tasks: Implement cost calculation for IBM Quantum (time-based)
Implement cost calculation for Amazon Braket (per-shot + per-task)
Implement cost calculation for Azure IonQ (per-gate)
Create comparison function that ranks providers by cost
Add support for free tier deductions
exercises.starterCode:
from dataclasses import dataclass
from typing import Dict, List, Optional
from enum import Enum

class Provider(Enum):
    IBM = 'ibm'
    BRAKET_IONQ = 'braket_ionq'
    BRAKET_RIGETTI = 'braket_rigetti'
    AZURE_IONQ = 'azure_ionq'

@dataclass
class JobSpec:
    shots: int
    circuit_depth: int
    num_qubits: int
    single_qubit_gates: int
    two_qubit_gates: int
    estimated_runtime_sec: Optional[float] = None

@dataclass
class CostBreakdown:
    provider: Provider
    base_cost: float
    variable_cost: float
    free_tier_discount: float
    total_cost: float

class QuantumCostCalculator:
    # Pricing constants
    IBM_PER_SECOND = 1.60
    BRAKET_IONQ_PER_SHOT = 0.03
    BRAKET_IONQ_BASE = 0.30
    BRAKET_RIGETTI_PER_SHOT = 0.00035
    BRAKET_RIGETTI_BASE = 0.30
    AZURE_IONQ_1Q_GATE = 0.00003
    AZURE_IONQ_2Q_GATE = 0.0003
    
    def __init__(self):
        self.free_tier_remaining = {
            Provider.IBM: 600,  # seconds
            Provider.AZURE_IONQ: 500  # dollars
        }
    
    def calculate_ibm(self, job: JobSpec) -> CostBreakdown:
        pass
    
    def calculate_braket_ionq(self, job: JobSpec) -> CostBreakdown:
        pass
    
    def calculate_braket_rigetti(self, job: JobSpec) -> CostBreakdown:
        pass
    
    def calculate_azure_ionq(self, job: JobSpec) -> CostBreakdown:
        pass
    
    def compare_all(self, job: JobSpec) -> List[CostBreakdown]:
        """Compare all providers, sorted by total cost."""
        pass
    
    def recommend_provider(self, job: JobSpec, 
                          constraints: Optional[Dict] = None) -> Provider:
        """Recommend best provider given constraints."""
        pass
exercises.expectedOutput: Cost calculator with accurate multi-provider comparison
hints: IBM: estimate runtime from circuit if not provided
Apply free tier before returning total

exercises.title: Shot Optimization Engine
exercises.description: Create an optimization engine that finds the optimal number of shots given precision requirements and budget constraints.
tasks: Calculate minimum shots for target precision using statistical formulas
Find maximum shots within a given budget
Implement precision-cost tradeoff analysis
Support adaptive shot allocation across multiple circuits
Generate Pareto frontier of precision vs cost options
exercises.starterCode:

exercises.expectedOutput: Shot optimization engine with precision-cost tradeoff analysis
hints: Rearrange for n: n = z^2 * p * (1-p) / precision^2
Pareto: no solution is better in both cost AND precision

exercises.title: Budget Management System
exercises.description: Build a comprehensive budget tracking and alerting system for quantum computing usage.
tasks: Track cumulative spending across providers
Implement threshold-based alerting (50%, 80%, 100%)
Project end-of-period spending from current rate
Generate usage reports by provider, time period, and job type
Implement spending limits that block jobs over budget
exercises.starterCode:
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from datetime import datetime, timedelta
from typing import Dict, List, Callable, Optional
import threading

@dataclass
class UsageRecord:
    timestamp: datetime
    provider: str
    job_id: str
    cost: float
    shots: int
    circuit_type: str

@dataclass
class BudgetConfig:
    monthly_limit: float
    alert_thresholds: List[float]  # e.g., [0.5, 0.8, 1.0]
    hard_limit: bool = True  # Block jobs over budget

class BudgetManager:
    def __init__(self, config: BudgetConfig):
        self.config = config
        self.records: List[UsageRecord] = []
        self.alerts_triggered: Dict[float, bool] = {t: False for t in config.alert_thresholds}
        self.alert_callbacks: List[Callable] = []
        self._lock = threading.Lock()
    
    def record_usage(self, provider: str, job_id: str, 
                    cost: float, shots: int, circuit_type: str) -> bool:
        """Record usage. Returns False if over budget and hard limit enabled."""
        pass
    
    def get_current_spend(self, 
                         period_start: Optional[datetime] = None) -> float:
        """Get total spend for current period."""
        pass
    
    def get_remaining_budget(self) -> float:
        """Get remaining budget for current period."""
        pass
    
    def check_alerts(self) -> List[float]:
        """Check and return newly triggered alert thresholds."""
        pass
    
    def project_spend(self, days_ahead: int = 30) -> Dict:
        """Project spending based on historical rate."""
        pass
    
    def get_report(self, 
                  group_by: str = 'provider') -> Dict:
        """Generate usage report grouped by provider/day/circuit_type."""
        pass
    
    def can_afford(self, estimated_cost: float) -> bool:
        """Check if estimated cost fits within remaining budget."""
        pass
    
    def register_alert_callback(self, callback: Callable) -> None:
        """Register callback for budget alerts."""
        pass
exercises.expectedOutput: Complete budget management system with alerting and projections
hints: Thread lock for concurrent usage recording
Trigger alerts only once per threshold

exercises.title: Cost-Aware Job Scheduler
exercises.description: Implement an intelligent job scheduler that minimizes costs while respecting deadlines and quality requirements.
tasks: Score providers based on cost, queue time, and fidelity
Implement deadline-aware scheduling (faster but more expensive if urgent)
Add quality-cost tradeoff (higher fidelity backends cost more)
Support batch optimization (schedule related jobs together)
Implement fallback logic when primary choice is unavailable
exercises.starterCode:
from dataclasses import dataclass
from datetime import datetime, timedelta
from typing import Dict, List, Optional, Tuple
import heapq

@dataclass
class SchedulingConstraints:
    max_cost: Optional[float] = None
    deadline: Optional[datetime] = None
    min_fidelity: Optional[float] = None
    preferred_providers: Optional[List[str]] = None

@dataclass
class ProviderStatus:
    name: str
    available: bool
    queue_time_minutes: float
    cost_per_shot: float
    two_qubit_fidelity: float

@dataclass
class SchedulingDecision:
    provider: str
    estimated_cost: float
    estimated_completion: datetime
    expected_fidelity: float
    reasoning: str

class CostAwareScheduler:
    def __init__(self, budget_manager):
        self.budget = budget_manager
        self.provider_status: Dict[str, ProviderStatus] = {}
    
    def update_provider_status(self, statuses: List[ProviderStatus]) -> None:
        """Update current provider availability and queue times."""
        pass
    
    def score_provider(self, provider: ProviderStatus,
                      job_cost: float,
                      constraints: SchedulingConstraints) -> float:
        """Score provider (higher is better) based on all factors."""
        pass
    
    def schedule_job(self, 
                    job_spec: Dict,
                    constraints: SchedulingConstraints) -> SchedulingDecision:
        """Schedule single job on optimal provider."""
        pass
    
    def schedule_batch(self,
                      jobs: List[Dict],
                      constraints: SchedulingConstraints) -> List[SchedulingDecision]:
        """Schedule batch of jobs, potentially grouping on same provider."""
        pass
    
    def get_cost_quality_options(self,
                                job_spec: Dict) -> List[SchedulingDecision]:
        """Return Pareto-optimal options trading cost vs quality."""
        pass
hints: Deadline: penalize if queue_time + exec_time > time_to_deadline
Batch: group jobs to amortize base costs
